title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Migration Coordination
summary: The image runs as root, startup mutates schema on every replica, readiness ignores PostgreSQL and backlog, shutdown drops leases, and rollout lacks version compatibility. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go migration coordination increment in deployable Go feed-service release unit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Run migrations through one owned release step with locks, timeout, compatibility gates, evidence, and explicit failure handling.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, degraded ingestion, backlog, database, and maintenance states without exposing secrets or causing restart loops.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Migration Coordination · Theory Feed Go Migration Coordinat…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7887 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run migrations through one owned release step with locks, timeout, compatibility gates, evidence, and explicit failure handling.
